text: GSH depletion is commonly observed in different types of organs and cells (Deneke and Fanburg 1989; Lushchak 2012; Aquilano, Baldelli, and Ciriolo 2014). One of the main roles of this antioxidant is to sequester free radicals in order to prevent cell damage. A decline in GSH levels has been thoroughly related to the increase of reactive oxygen species, as well as to lipid peroxides, culminating in tissue oxidative stress  (Comporti et al. 1991; Martin and Teismann 2009; Lushchak 2012; Aquilano, Baldelli, and Ciriolo 2014).
